title: spiraea crenata
intro: Spiraea crenata, also known as creeping spirea, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and small, toothed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Spiraea crenata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Spiraea crenata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
